sections.heading: Architecture Overview
sections.body: The i18n system uses @solid-primitives/i18n v2.2.1 with flat dot-notation dictionaries. Dictionaries are stored as flat string-keyed objects in each locale file. The app merges two dictionaries at runtime: the app's own locale (packages/app/src/i18n/) and the UI kit's locale (packages/ui/src/i18n/). The merged dictionary is then flattened (for type safety, though the values are already flat) and passed through i18n.translator() which creates a reactive translation function t('dot.key.path', params).

sections.heading: Dependency: @solid-primitives/i18n v2.2.1
sections.body: Located at packages/app/node_modules/@solid-primitives/i18n/. Version 2.2.1, ESM module, peer dependency on solid-js ^1.6.12. Ten exported symbols: flatten, prefix, template, resolveTemplate, identityResolveTemplate, translator, scopedTranslator, chainedTranslator, proxyTranslator, and type utilities (Flatten, Translator, Resolver, Template, BaseDict).

details.flatten: Converts nested dict to flat dot-notation dict. Keeps original nested entries alongside flattened paths. Example: flatten({a:{foo:'hello'}}) returns {a:{foo:'hello'}, 'a.foo':'hello'}.
details.resolveTemplate: Replaces {{ key }} placeholders with values from an args object using global regex replacement. Handles whitespace inside braces.
details.translator: Creates a lookup function from a reactive getter (() => dict). Dispatches by value type: calls function values with args, resolves string values through resolveTemplate with args, returns other values as-is.
details.scopedTranslator: Wraps a translator to prefix all lookups (scoped('key') → t('scope.key')).
details.chainedTranslator: Creates a nested object of callable functions mirroring the dict structure.
details.proxyTranslator: Proxy-based chained translator for dynamic resolution.

sections.heading: Source-of-Truth: en.ts
sections.body: File: packages/app/src/i18n/en.ts — 1072 lines, exports a flat object literal with 963 dot-notation string keys. Values are English strings, some containing {{ param }} template placeholders. Structure is NOT a nested JS object — all keys are quoted strings with dots, up to 5 levels deep.

sections.heading: UI Package i18n (Merged)
sections.body: The packages/ui/src/i18n/en.ts file contributes 152 additional keys that are merged at runtime via: const base = i18n.flatten({ ...en, ...uiEn }). These keys are prefixed with 'ui.' (UI component labels, session review, tool names, permissions, etc.) and 'dialog.usageExceeded.' (rate limit dialogs). The app's dict type is defined as: type RawDictionary = typeof en & typeof uiEn.

sections.heading: Runtime Architecture
sections.body: The i18n system is wired through a SolidJS context provider (LanguageProvider) defined in packages/app/src/context/language.tsx (243 lines).

setup.base_dict: i18n.flatten({ ...en, ...uiEn }) creates the base Dictionary type
setup.caching: Map<Locale, Dictionary> caches loaded dictionaries per locale
setup.lazy_loading: createResource(locale, loadDict) loads locale on demand via dynamic import()
setup.merge: For non-English locales: merge(import('@/i18n/xx'), import('@opencode-ai/ui/i18n/xx')) combines app + UI, then flattens
setup.translator: const t = i18n.translator(() => dict() ?? base, i18n.resolveTemplate) — reactive getter + template resolver
setup.typed_as: (key: keyof Dictionary, params?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>) => string

key_features: All 18 locale dictionaries are pre-loaded for the 'en' locale; others loaded on first access
Thermal pre-warming: if stored locale is non-English, starts loading it immediately
Locale detection via navigator.languages with 18 ISO matchers

sections.heading: Locale Parity Report
sections.body: All 17 non-English locale files are missing keys compared to en.ts (963 keys). The following table summarizes the gap for each locale.

details.methodology: Keys extracted via regex from each locale file's dict object. Missing keys identified by set subtraction against en.ts key set.

universally_missing_keys.note: The following keys are missing from ALL 17 non-English locales

by_group_gaps.command: 5 keys missing from all locales except uk (2 missing). Missing: palette.empty, palette.search.placeholder, project.index/next/previous.
by_group_gaps.dialog: 37-49 keys missing from all. Both dialog.agents (18 keys) and dialog.mcp (19 keys) are entirely untranslated in every non-English locale.
by_group_gaps.github: All 13 keys missing from ALL locales. Entire GitHub integration is English-only.
by_group_gaps.home: 18 keys missing from all except zh (1 missing). Home page filtering/session UI is mostly untranslated.
by_group_gaps.session: 7 keys missing from all (child.backToParent, child.promptDisabled, new.project.*, review.*). uk has 3 missing.
by_group_gaps.settings.general: 21 keys missing from all (newLayoutDesigns, pinchZoom, shell, showCustomAgents, showFileTree, showNavigation, showSearch, showStatus, showTerminal, section.advanced). uk has 6 missing.
by_group_gaps.sidebar: 2 keys missing from all: empty.description, empty.title
by_group_gaps.toast.session: 9 keys missing from bs, da, es, no, ru, th, tr, uk, zht (session export/import toasts). Not missing from other locales.
by_group_gaps.language.uk: language.uk key is missing from all locales — ironic since this is the label for the Ukrainian language itself

locale_specific_anomalies.uk: Has all command.session keys (no missing command.project.*), has all session keys (child, review), all settings keys — more complete than most
locale_specific_anomalies.zh: Only missing 1 home key (home.sessions.empty.description) — most complete home page translation
locale_specific_anomalies.bs, da, no, th, tr, zht: Missing all 9 toast.session keys on top of the common gaps

sections.heading: Technical Observations
sections.body: Key architectural details about the i18n implementation.
items: The dict is exported as a flat object literal (export const dict = { 'key': 'value', ... }). Dots in keys are literal, not representing JS property access.
Despite the flat storage, the flatten() function from @solid-primitives/i18n is still called — it's a no-op for flat dicts but provides type-level Flatten<T> mapping for TypeScript.
The Flatten type utility creates a type where both the nested path AND dot-path are valid keys (e.g., both 'session' and 'session.header.open.app.vscode' are valid GenericTranslator keys).
The resolveTemplate is always passed as the second argument to translator(), meaning ALL string lookups go through template resolution — even strings without {{ }} pass through as-is.
Locales are cached in a Map<Locale, Dictionary> after first load — subsequent switches are instant after the first access.

The parity.test.ts only tests 2 specific keys (command.session.previous.unseen, command.session.next.unseen) to ensure they're translated (not equal to English). This is a minimal smoke test, not comprehensive coverage.
